macro_events.event_summary: Trump swears in Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair, signaling a clear push toward lower interest rates.
primary_drivers: Lower expected policy rate path / dovish Fed leadership
Lower discount rate boosts long-duration asset valuations
Weaker USD expectations from looser policy stance
Steeper yield curve potential benefiting financials' NIM on new originations

beneficiaries.rationale: Homebuilders are among the most rate-sensitive equities — Warsh's dovish mandate would lower mortgage rates, directly unlocking pent-up housing demand. D.R. Horton has the largest entry-level exposure.
Weaker dollar + commodity reflation trade from a dovish Fed historically lifts hard commodity producers; coal/metals miners are leveraged plays on this rotation.
High-multiple growth name with extreme duration sensitivity; rate-cut narratives have historically driven outsized PLTR rallies via multiple expansion.
Charles Schwab's bond portfolio losses compress as long rates fall and deposit outflows from cash-sorting reverse when short rates drop; double benefit from a dovish chair.

losers.rationale: Large money-center banks face NIM compression as the front end falls faster than long end stabilizes; JPM specifically has the largest asset-sensitive balance sheet exposure to falling short rates.
Life insurers depend on reinvesting at higher long-term yields to fund annuity liabilities; a dovish Fed pushing the entire curve lower pressures reinvestment spreads.

headlines: Watch: Trump swearing in Kevin Warsh as Fed chair as he seeks interest rate cuts

macro_events.event_summary: AI server makers rally sharply with Dell +15%, HPE +9%, and Super Micro +5% ahead of earnings, signaling renewed enthusiasm for AI infrastructure spending.
primary_drivers: Reacceleration of hyperscaler AI capex / server orders
Read-through to AI infrastructure supply chain (GPUs, networking, power, cooling, memory)
Sentiment/positioning rotation back into AI hardware names ahead of catalysts

beneficiaries.rationale: Already moving (+5%) but a direct peer to DELL/HPE in AI server assembly; any read-through on hyperscaler GPU server demand flows straight into SMCI's order book given >60% AI server revenue mix.

Dell/HPE/SMCI AI server rally implies stronger pull-through of H100/H200/GB200 GPUs — Nvidia silicon is the dominant BOM component (~50-70% of AI server cost). Server OEM strength is the cleanest demand-side tell for NVDA.
AI server build-out drives demand for Broadcom's networking ASICs (Tomahawk/Jericho) and custom accelerators (Google TPU, Meta MTIA). Server unit growth maps directly to networking silicon attach.
Arista's 400G/800G Ethernet switches are the dominant back-end fabric for AI clusters built on Dell/HPE/SMCI racks. Server shipment acceleration = switch attach acceleration.

Vertiv supplies liquid cooling and power distribution for the exact AI racks Dell/HPE/SMCI ship. Each AI server rack drives ~5-10x the cooling/power content of traditional servers — direct unit-volume read-through.
AI servers carry 6-8x the DRAM and HBM content of standard servers. Stronger Dell/HPE AI server shipments tighten HBM3e demand where Micron is gaining share against Samsung/Hynix.
Credo's Active Electrical Cables (AECs) are designed into hyperscaler AI racks for intra-rack connectivity; AI server unit growth at Dell/HPE is a direct AEC demand signal.

losers.rationale: Legacy enterprise server franchise (Power/Z mainframe) loses mindshare and IT budget when AI server capex dominates the spend mix; not a direct loser on the print but a relative-rotation funding source.
Traditional enterprise storage faces budget reallocation toward AI compute (Dell/HPE servers + Pure/VAST for AI storage); NTAP is the relative laggard in the AI storage narrative versus PSTG.

headlines: Dell Surges 15% Leading AI Server Rally; HPE Pops 9%, Super Micro Rises 5% Ahead of Earnings Catalyst

macro_events.event_summary: A sharp rise in Treasury yields is pushing bond investors to rotate out of "risk-free" government debt into higher-yielding or higher-return alternatives.
primary_drivers: Higher discount rate compresses long-duration equity valuations
Net interest margin expansion for banks/insurers with asset-sensitive balance sheets
Capital rotation from Treasuries into credit, dividend equities, and alternatives

beneficiaries.rationale: Schwab earns a large share of revenue from net interest on client cash sweep balances; higher front-end yields directly expand spread income and reduce the cash sorting headwind that hurt it in prior cycles.
Interactive Brokers earns ~50%+ of revenue from net interest on margin loans and customer cash; rising yields flow almost directly to the bottom line with minimal deposit beta.
Life insurer with large fixed-income reinvestment book; higher yields on new money lift portfolio yields and widen spread on annuity/GA products. Direct earnings tailwind.
P&C insurer with sizeable investment portfolio rolling into higher-yielding Treasuries/corporates; investment income line gets a direct boost as the float reinvests.
Asset-sensitive money-center bank benefits from higher reinvestment yields on securities book and loan repricing, partially offset by deposit beta — but on net NII rises with the curve.
Investors fleeing 'risk-free' into 'better opportunities' is exactly the alt-credit/private credit pitch; Blackstone's BCRED and direct lending platforms benefit from rotation flows.

losers.rationale: Realty Income — net-lease REIT explicitly traded as a bond substitute; dividend yield must reprice wider versus the 10Y, and AFFO is pressured by higher refinancing costs.
Homebuilder directly hit by higher mortgage rates suppressing affordability and order volume; rate-sensitivity is the dominant earnings driver.
Highly levered consumer balance sheet with floor-plan financing and securitization-dependent business model; higher yields raise funding costs and squeeze auto loan affordability for subprime buyers.

headlines: Surge in 'risk-free' treasury yields sends bond investors in search of better opportunities

macro_events.event_summary: Commentary piece on what Ross, TJX, and Walmart earnings reveal about the US consumer — no specific data point disclosed in the headline itself.
primary_drivers: Off-price and discount retailer commentary as a read-through on consumer health
headlines: Ross, TJX, and Walmart reveal how the real engine of the U.S. economy is doing

macro_events.event_summary: US equities rally with the Dow hitting a record high as markets focus on diplomatic US-Iran talks, raising prospects of de-escalation in the Middle East.
primary_drivers: Potential easing of Iran sanctions could unlock ~1-1.5mbpd of Iranian crude supply, pressuring oil prices
Lower geopolitical risk premium reduces defense spending narrative and safe-haven demand
Risk-on rotation into cyclicals as Middle East tail risk diminishes

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline operating costs. Iran deal that pressures Brent lower directly expands margins; Delta has highest leverage among legacy carriers with limited fuel hedging.
Same fuel-cost pass-through mechanism as DAL; United runs largely unhedged and benefits disproportionately from a sustained crude pullback on Iran supply normalization.
Southwest historically hedged but rolling off; lower jet fuel from increased Iranian supply flows directly to operating margin given domestic-route fuel intensity.
Naphtha/ethane feedstock costs track crude; lower oil from Iran supply return improves chemical spreads. Cyclical chemicals also benefit from risk-on tape.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with significant upstream exposure; Iran deal returning ~1mbpd to global market pressures Brent and compresses upstream cash flow. Largest weight in oil-price-sensitive megacap energy.
Pure-play E&P with near-100% earnings sensitivity to crude prices; no downstream offset to cushion an Iran-supply-driven price decline.
Highly levered Permian pure-play E&P; crude price drops from Iranian barrels returning amplify equity moves due to balance sheet leverage post-Anadarko.
Middle East de-escalation narrative compresses defense multiples; Lockheed has direct exposure to regional FMS demand (THAAD, PAC-3, F-35 sales to Gulf allies tied to Iran threat).
Same de-escalation peer rotation; Northrop's missile defense and B-21 narrative softens if Iran tail risk fades.

headlines: Wall St climbs, Dow at record high with US-Iran talks in spotlight
